questions.question: What does a large language model fundamentally do?
options: Searches the internet for answers
Predicts the most likely next token based on training data
Runs logical rules defined by programmers
Accesses a live database of facts

questions.question: What is a 'hallucination' in the context of AI?
options: When the AI refuses to answer
When the AI generates plausible-sounding but incorrect information
When the AI repeats itself
When the AI asks for clarification

questions.question: Which best describes the difference between narrow AI and AGI?
options: Narrow AI is cheaper; AGI is expensive
Narrow AI performs specific tasks; AGI would perform any intellectual task a human can
Narrow AI uses GPUs; AGI uses CPUs
Narrow AI is open source; AGI is proprietary

questions.question: Why do language models sometimes produce confident wrong answers?
options: They are programmed to be confident
They lack access to the internet
They optimise for plausibility, not truth — they cannot verify facts
They have too little training data

questions.question: What did the transformer architecture (2017) fundamentally change?
options: It made AI cheaper to run
It enabled models to weigh the relevance of all words in context simultaneously
It removed the need for training data
It allowed AI to reason about images
